QF.id: XC Investment Products and Market Developments_5

question.text: A Systematic Investment Plan (SIP) offers which of the following advantages to an investor?

answer.text: Guaranteed returns higher than fixed deposits
Rupee cost averaging and disciplined investing over time
Tax-free returns regardless of the investment period
Elimination of all market risk through regular investments

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SIP's primary advantages are rupee cost averaging (buying more units when prices are low and fewer when high) and instilling discipline in regular investing. **The Trap:** SIPs don't guarantee returns, eliminate market risk, or provide tax-free status. They reduce the impact of market timing but don't eliminate market risk. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series V-A extensively tests SIP concepts. Series X-A covers SIP as an investment mode. Series XVII recommends SIPs for retirement accumulation. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** SIPs work best over longer periods (7+ years) — show clients the rupee cost averaging effect using actual historical data to build conviction for staying invested through market cycles.
